title: Socket Hang Up Error with Google Gemini Node
symptom: When using the Google Gemini Node with a socket proxy, the error 'socket hang up' occurs exactly at 60 seconds. This prevents the node from completing requests that take longer than this duration.
not_this_if: Streaming is enabled in the Gemini node
root_cause: The error occurs due to the interaction between the Node.js HTTP client and the idle connection timeout of the socket proxy. When the request takes longer than 60 seconds without data transmission, the proxy closes the connection, resulting in a 'socket hang up' error.
fix_steps: Increase the idle connection timeout in the ClashX/Clash Verge configuration to a value greater than 60 seconds.
Alternatively, switch to using the HTTP Request node to call the Gemini REST API directly, setting the timeout to a higher value (e.g., 120000ms).
Consider enabling streaming in the Google Gemini Node if applicable.
agent_instruction: Adjust the socket proxy timeout settings or use the HTTP Request node to call the Gemini API directly.
